name: ShadowStep
summary: **ShadowStep** is a professional-grade, modular toolkit for **artifact management**, **metadata manipulation**, and **system sanitization**. Designed for security professionals and Red Team operators, it provides a cooperative suite of utilities to automate cover-track workflows in authorized, legal, and controlled environments.

documentation.readme: ShadowStep is a professional-grade, modular toolkit for artifact management, metadata manipulation, and system sanitization. Designed for security professionals and Red Team operators, it provides a cooperative suite of utilities to automate cover-track workflows in authorized, legal, and controlled environments.
usage_sections: Modular Architecture: Clear separation of responsibilities across core modules (Janitor, Surgeon, Cleaner).
Cross-Platform: Native support for Windows, Linux, and macOS with intelligent OS detection and safe fallbacks.

arguments.name: -c
arguments.description: Clear system traces (Shell History, Clipboard, DNS, RAM & Swap)

arguments.name: --clean
arguments.description: Clear system traces (Shell History, Clipboard, DNS, RAM & Swap)

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: Show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: Show this help message and exit

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: Network interface to operate on (Default: eth0)

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to operate on (Default: eth0)

arguments.name: -k
arguments.description: Keywords to remove from logs (e.g., --keywords 192.168.1.5 admin)

arguments.name: --keywords
arguments.description: Keywords to remove from logs (e.g., --keywords 192.168.1.5 admin)

arguments.name: -M
arguments.description: Manual MAC address to set (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55)

arguments.name: -m
arguments.description: Change the MAC address of a network interface

arguments.name: --mac
arguments.description: Manual MAC address to set (e.g., 00:11:22:33:44:55)

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: Number of shredder passes (Default: 3)

arguments.name: --passes
arguments.description: Number of shredder passes (Default: 3)

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: Reference file to copy timestamps from (for --timestomp)

arguments.name: --ref
arguments.description: Reference file to copy timestamps from (for --timestomp)

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: Securely delete a file (DoD 5220.22-M)

arguments.name: --sanitize
arguments.description: Surgically remove keywords from log files (or Event Logs on Windows)

arguments.name: --shred
arguments.description: Securely delete a file (DoD 5220.22-M)

arguments.name: --spoof
arguments.description: Change the MAC address of a network interface

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: Change target file timestamps

arguments.name: --timestomp
arguments.description: Change target file timestamps

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install shadowstep; brew install shadowstep. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `___/ / / / / /_/ / /_/ / /_/ / |/ |/ / ___/ / /_/  __/ /_/ /`. Further invocations from upstream: `brew tap s4l1hs/homebrew-shadowstep`; `shadowstep --shred confidential.txt --passes 7`. ShadowStep is a professional-grade, modular toolkit for artifact management, metadata manipulation, and system sanitization. Designed for security professionals and Red Team operators, it provides a cooperative suite of utilities to automate cover-track workflows in authorized, legal, and controlled environments.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [shell_linux] in the Penetration Testing category. Fits when the engagement needs: **ShadowStep** is a professional-grade, modular toolkit for **artifact management**, **metadata manipulation**, and **system sanitization**. Designed for security professionals and Red Team operators, it provides a coope.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: ShadowStep is a professional-grade, modular toolkit for artifact management, metadata manipulation, and system sanitization. Designed for security professionals and Red Team operators, it provides a cooperative suite of utilities to automate cover-track workflows in authorized, legal, and controlled environments.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with androguard/androguard (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with MythicAgents/nemesis (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with volatilityfoundation/volatility3 (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['shell_linux', 'shell_windows', 'web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='exploit'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: Use during the active exploitation phase once recon has identified a service or surface on the engagement host.
Use as a fallback when the primary exploit path (CVE match, KB hit) did not yield a shell.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
